Meeting discussions become Linear issues
automatically

contextprompt joins your engineering meetings on Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams, transcribes the conversation, scans your connected repositories, and extracts structured Linear-ready issues with real file paths and implementation steps — your cycle backlog fills itself.

Try it free

No API keys. Free plan includes 1 hour/month of recording.

Why fast-moving teams still lose time to Linear triaging

Linear is built for speed. Manual issue creation from meetings is the bottleneck that slows everything down.

//

Issues created hours after the meeting

Engineering teams using Linear for velocity still create issues manually after every planning session. Between the meeting ending and the issues appearing in the cycle, the context and urgency evaporate — and the person creating them often isn't the person who'll implement them.

?!

Linear issues lack file-level context

A Linear issue with "add pagination to the search results" gives developers no starting point. Which component renders search results? Which API endpoint returns them? Which pagination library does the codebase already use? Without file paths, every issue starts with an investigation.

~>

Cycle backlog never reflects meeting output

Fast teams plan quickly but issue creation is slow. Linear cycles start with half the issues from the planning meeting still unwritten. Developers pick work off the top of the backlog instead of the most important thing the team just agreed on.

Linear issues that match what your team decided.

contextprompt cross-references your meeting transcript with your codebase to produce developer-ready Linear issues, immediately.

<5m

from meeting end to issues

100%

of meeting decisions captured

0

manual issue writes

Cycle-ready issues, immediately

As soon as your planning meeting ends, contextprompt produces structured issues for every task discussed. Title, description, implementation steps, and file paths — ready to drag into your current Linear cycle without editing.

Exact file paths from your repos

contextprompt scans your connected repositories before extraction. Every Linear issue arrives with the specific file paths that need to change — so developers can open the right file instead of searching for it.

Works with any team workflow

Whether you run structured sprints or async cycles, contextprompt output drops cleanly into Linear's issue body. Edit before adding, or add as-is — the output is structured markdown that fits any Linear workflow.

What a contextprompt Linear issue looks like

Actual structured output from a cycle planning meeting. Drop it directly into a Linear issue description.

contextprompt → Linear Issue cycle-ready
ENG-412
Implement client-side feature flag evaluation for the new onboarding flow
Status: Backlog
Priority: High
Cycle: Cycle 18

In cycle planning (2026-04-28), Priya proposed gating the new onboarding flow behind a feature flag so the team can roll out to 5% of users first. Tom confirmed the flagging library (flagsmith) is already installed. Decision: evaluate the flag client-side in the Layout component so the flag check doesn't block the initial render.

src/components/Layout.tsx src/hooks/useFeatureFlag.ts src/pages/Onboarding.tsx
Implementation steps
  • Create src/hooks/useFeatureFlag.ts — wraps flagsmith.isFeatureEnabled(), returns bool
  • Import useFeatureFlag in src/components/Layout.tsx, call with 'new-onboarding-flow' key
  • Conditionally render <Onboarding /> vs <OnboardingLegacy /> based on flag value
  • Add flag to flagsmith dashboard: 'new-onboarding-flow', default off, rollout 5%
  • Add unit test: mock flag true/false, assert correct component renders
Confidence: high  ·  Source: cycle planning 2026-04-28, 11:43 (Priya S., Tom K.)

From planning meeting to Linear in 4 steps

No plugins, no Linear integration setup, no audio configuration. Works in 2 minutes.

1
One-time setup

Connect your repositories

Add your project repos from the contextprompt dashboard. The tool indexes your file tree, exported functions, and TypeScript types to build the repo map it uses to attach real file paths to every extracted issue.

2
Cycle planning starts

Paste the meeting link

Drop any Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams link into contextprompt. The AI bot joins your planning meeting as a named participant and records the full conversation with per-speaker transcription.

3
Meeting ends

AI extracts cycle issues

contextprompt cross-references the meeting transcript against your repo map. Every task discussed is extracted with title, description, implementation steps, file paths, and a confidence level. Duplicate tasks across the meeting are merged automatically.

4
Minutes after the meeting

Copy into Linear or hand to an AI assistant

Issues appear in the contextprompt dashboard. Copy any issue's structured output into a Linear issue body — or hand it to Claude Code, Cursor, or another AI coding assistant and start implementing without any setup.

★★★★★
“Meeting tools don’t understand code. Coding agents don’t attend meetings. contextprompt is the glue that was missing. Our Linear cycles now start full instead of half-empty.”
Sarah K. — Senior Developer

Frequently asked questions

Everything you need to know about contextprompt and Linear.

Does contextprompt push issues directly into Linear?

Not automatically yet — contextprompt extracts structured issues with file paths and steps, which you copy into a Linear issue description. Direct Linear API push (creating issues in your workspace automatically) is on the roadmap. In practice, the copy flow takes under a minute per issue.

Which meeting platforms are supported?

Zoom, Google Meet, Slack Huddles, and Microsoft Teams are all supported. Paste the meeting link — the bot joins as a named participant, no browser extension or audio driver required.

How does contextprompt know which files to reference in Linear issues?

You connect your repositories from the dashboard. contextprompt scans each repo's file tree, exported functions, and TypeScript types, then cross-references the meeting discussion against this map to attach the most relevant file paths to each extracted issue — no hallucinated paths.

Does it work for async teams or only sync planning calls?

contextprompt works for any synchronous meeting where tasks are discussed — planning calls, standups, architecture reviews, incident post-mortems. It doesn't currently process async Loom videos or written Slack threads, only live meeting recordings.

Can I use contextprompt if my team uses Linear without Jira?

Yes — contextprompt is tracker-agnostic. The output is structured markdown that you paste wherever you track work. There's no requirement to use Jira, GitHub Issues, or any specific project management tool alongside Linear.
